seoTitle: Port Douglas vs Port Of Brisbane — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Douglas and Port Of Brisbane. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Douglas vs Port Of Brisbane: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Douglas and Port Of Brisbane.
heading: Port Douglas vs Port Of Brisbane — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Douglas, Queensland and Port Of Brisbane, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Douglas has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Port Of Brisbane (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Douglas or Port Of Brisbane safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Douglas has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Port Of Brisbane scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Douglas scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Port Of Brisbane scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Douglas or Port Of Brisbane?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Douglas has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
